args.query: Tesla 2018 2019 Model 3 front suspension recall issue

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Front Suspension Lateral Link\
  \ May Separate
- url: https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/rcl/2023/RCAK-23V235-7009.pdf
\
  - source: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration
- domain: static.nhtsa.gov
\
  
## Content

# Front Suspension Lateral Link May Separate

**Date:** April\
  \ 6, 2023  
**NHTSA Campaign Number:** 23V-235

This notice acknowledges Tesla,\
  \ Inc.'s notification to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration of a\
  \ safety recall conducted under Federal law.

## Makes, Models and Model Years
\
  
- TESLA / MODEL 3 / 2018-2019

**Manufacturer Report Date:** March 31, 2023
\
  
**Components:**
- Suspension: Critical Fasteners
- Suspension: Front

**Potential\
  \ Number of Units Affected:** 422

## Problem Description

Tesla, Inc. is recalling\
  \ certain 2018-2019 Model 3 vehicles. The front suspension lateral link fasteners\
  \ may loosen, allowing the lateral link to separate from the sub-frame.

## Consequence
\
  
A lateral link separation could shift the wheel alignment, causing instability\
  \ and increasing the risk of a crash.

## Remedy

Tesla Service will tighten\
  \ or replace the lateral link fasteners as necessary, free of charge. Owner notification\
  \ letters were expected to be mailed May 30, 2023.

Tesla's number for this recall\
  \ is SB-23-31-001. This recall is an expansion of previous recall number 21V-835.
\
  
Owners may contact Tesla customer service at 1-877-798-3752. Owners may also contact\
  \ the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration Vehicle Safety Hotline at 1-888-327-4236.
"

I checked the vehicle linked to your account: **2019 Tesla Model 3, VIN ending 17642**.

- **Front suspension recall — NHTSA 23V-235 / Tesla SB-23-31-001:** **Not open** for your vehicle.
- This recall involved certain 2018–2019 Model 3 vehicles whose front suspension lateral-link fasteners could loosen. Tesla’s remedy was to inspect and tighten or replace the fasteners, free of charge.  
  Source: [NHTSA recall notice](https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/rcl/2023/RCAK-23V235-7009.pdf)
- **Another safety recall, NHTSA 24V-554, is currently open** for your vehicle. The vehicle record does not provide the issue or remedy details.

**What you need to do:** You do **not** need recall service now for the front suspension issue. However, you should arrange service for the separate open recall through the Tesla app or Tesla’s [VIN Recall Search](https://service.tesla.com/en-US/vin-recall-search). Recall repairs are generally free. If you notice unusual steering, wheel-alignment changes, instability, or suspension noises, stop driving if unsafe and contact Tesla service.

final_answer:
I checked the vehicle linked to your account: **2019 Tesla Model 3, VIN ending 17642**.

- **Front suspension recall — NHTSA 23V-235 / Tesla SB-23-31-001:** **Not open** for your vehicle.
- This recall involved certain 2018–2019 Model 3 vehicles whose front suspension lateral-link fasteners could loosen. Tesla’s remedy was to inspect and tighten or replace the fasteners, free of charge.  
  Source: [NHTSA recall notice](https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/rcl/2023/RCAK-23V235-7009.pdf)
- **Another safety recall, NHTSA 24V-554, is currently open** for your vehicle. The vehicle record does not provide the issue or remedy details.

**What you need to do:** You do **not** need recall service now for the front suspension issue. However, you should arrange service for the separate open recall through the Tesla app or Tesla’s [VIN Recall Search](https://service.tesla.com/en-US/vin-recall-search). Recall repairs are generally free. If you notice unusual steering, wheel-alignment changes, instability, or suspension noises, stop driving if unsafe and contact Tesla service.
